def: A colorless, crystalline, aromatic hydrocarbon, C6H2(CH3)4, offartificial production, with an odor like camphor.
The hypothetical radical C4H3S, regarded as the essentialresidue of thiophene and certain of its derivatives.
An animal having broad feet, or a broad foot.
To shoot upward. "Trees upshooting high." Spenser.

A word of unknown origin and signification, formerly used asexpressive of contempt, or when anything said was reject as triflingor impertinent. [Written also tille-vally, tilly-fally, tille-fally,and otherwise.] Shak.
One who, or that which, incloses; one who fences off land fromcommon grounds.
Having leaves like those of the vine; ornamented with vineleaves. "Vined and figured columns." Sir H. Wotton.
A mode of speech peculiar to the Turks; a Turkish idiom orexpression; also, in general, a Turkish mode or custom.
A lesson from the Torah, or Law, from which at least onesection is read in the Jewish synagogue on every Sabbath andfestival.
Pertaining to, or characterized by, insurrection; rebellious;seditious.Their murderous insurrectionary system. Burke.
The endmost plank of a strake which reaches the stem or stern.
